def: oblique plural of بھاشَن (bhāśan, “speech; lecture”)
adj. Loose, flabby, flaccid; soft; weak ( = phasphasā; pilpilā).
adj. Flabby, loose (as meat).
s.f. Language, speech; the vernacular of any country; dialect; the Hindī language; a word; plaint at law; an epithet of Sarasvatī (the goddess of speech); one of the rāginīs or musical modes
subs: bhāshātmik (bhāshā + ātmaka) = bhāshā-bandh, adj. Written in a vernacular.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit भाषा (bhāṣā).

def: s.m. Speaking, talking, saying; speech, talk; language.
ex: اَپْنا یِہ بھاشَن کِسی اَور کو دینا۔
apnā ye bhāśan kisī aur ko denā.
Give this lecture to someone else.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit भाषण (bhāṣaṇa).

def: part. & s.m. Spoken, uttered, said; — speech, utterance.
s.m. Speaking, talking; any work in the common or vernacular speech; explanatory work, exposition, commentary.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit भाष्य (bhāṣya).

def: s.f. A species of beet, Beta Bengalensis.
adj. & s.m. Light-causing, shining, glittering, resplendent; — the sun; fire; gold; a hero; name of a famous Hindū astronomer (commonly called Bhāskarāćārya); the plant Calotropis gigantea.
more: s. m. 1. The sun. 2. Name of a Hindū astronomer, author of the Siddhānta Shiromani and other works.

def: s.f. Ashes, cinders
more: n. m. bhasmī , n. f. S. भसम from भस् to consume. Ashes. bhasam asnān . The act of smearing the body with the ashes of cow-dung. bhasam-patrī , n. f. A cant term for gāṅjhā the hemp plant. Lāo to bhasam patrī, Na bache bāman, na bache chhatrī . Bring now the hempen liquor strong, Brahman nor Kshatrī shall resist it long. bhasam tikkā , n. m. Burnt flesh or meat. bhasam ramānā , v. a. To rub ashes on the body. Sakhī rī, nahīṅ chandan, nahīṅ ketkī, nahīṅ phūl sugandh, Yeh bhauṅrā Brij-bāulā, bhasam ramāwe aṅg . Dohā. My dear, not sandal wood, nor ketkī , nor sweet-scented flower, This beetle mad rolls in the ashes of his love each hour. bhasam kar'nā , v. a. To turn or reduce to ashes; calcine; burn. Yār ko karūṅ pyār, khasam ko karūṅ bhasam, laṛke ko karūṅ chaṭnī . Prov. My gallant I'll love, my spouse to ashes burn, my children all to pickle turn. bhasam honā , v. n. 1. To be reduced to a…
subs: (S. bhasmasnān), s.m. The act of smearing the body with the ashes of cow-dung
s.m. A certain demon who had the power to reduce to ashes any one on whose head he placed his hand
s.f. The hemp plant; gāṅjhā (a cant term)
s.m. Meat burnt or roasted on cinders, chop, &c. = bhas-tikkā, q.v.
v.n. To rub ashes on the body
v.t. To reduce to ashes; to burn, calcine
s.f. The sissoo-tree, Dalbergia sissoo, or a variety of it
v.n. To be reduced to ashes; to be digested, consumed, utterly destroyed, &c.; to be overcome with rage, be in a great rage.

def: s.m. Morbid appetite with general decay; disease of the eyes, indistinctness of vision (arising from a thickening of the membranes)
s.m. What is reduced to ashes; ashes; anything utterly consumed or destroyed; any perishing thing.
s.f. Ashes; ashes of a corpse; — place of cremation
more: n. f. 1. The ashes of the corpse. 2. The place of cremation.
subs: s.m. The act of reducing to ashes; completely consuming or burning; calcining
part. Reduced to ashes calcined (as a metal).

def: v.n. To be manifest, to appear, be known.
more: v. n. To be known, to appear.

def: v.n. To float, float away, be carried down the stream; to go down, sink; to be drowned. (In some of its meanings the word would seem to point to the S. rt. vah, as its source).
more: v. n. 1. To go down the stream. 2. To sink; fall or go down; to be drowned. 1. Bālū kī bhīt bhas gaī . 2. Ūṅṭ, ghoṛā bhas gaël, gadhā pūchhe kitnā pānī ? E. Prov. Horse and camel drowned, the donkey asks, is it deep?

def: s. m. A steak, chop, collop.
s.f. Bellows ( = bhaṭhī).
s.m. Husband's eldest brother, brother-in-law.
more: s. m. A husbandʼs elder brother.

def: adj. & s.m. Shining, splendid; — crystal; a hero; Costus speciosus or Arabicus.
v.t. = bhasānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to float, to set afloat, to launch.
adj. & s.m. Shining, radiant, &c. ( = bhāsur); — the sun, daylight; day; — Costus speciosus or Arabicus.

s.m. Advances to ploughmen without interest, &c. = bhātā and bhattā, qq.v.); — boiled rice; food; — a marriage ceremony which consists in the mother's family presenting rice, mūṅg, garments, money, &c. to the bride (generally on the second day's feast); — name of a soil to the north of the Ganges (that retains its humidity for a long time, and contains a large quantity of nitre); uneven ground
more: n. m. S. भक्तम, Pr. bhatto . 1. Boiled rice; food. Bhūkā Baṅgālī 'bhāt, bhāt' pukāre . Prov. The hungry Bengālī cries out, 'rice, rice.' 2. A ceremony in which rice, mūṅg , garments, money, etc. are presented to the bride or bridegroom by the maternal grandfather. mātā kā bhāt . An offering of boiled rice made to a goddess.
n. m. bhāṭan , n. f. S. भट्ट from भट to speak. 1. A bard; minstrel; troubadour; a chronicler of ancient days; genealogist. 2. Met . A panegyrist; eulogist; flatterer.
subs: s.m. The invitation to the grand-parents of the bride to attend the ceremony of bhāt and to make the usual presents (generally given by the bride's mother).

def: s.m. Dawn, early morning.
s.m. Name of a particular mixed caste of hereditary panegyrists; bard, minstrel, panegyrist, genealogist or family bard; eulogist; flatterer.
more: s. m. Name of a tribe, a bard. See بندي and ماگده.

def: s.f. Fire-place, oven, furnace, kiln ( = bhaṭṭhā); pit; hole (especially of an animal, as a fox, &c.), cave, lair, den; — misfortune; curse
more: s. f. 1. An oven, a furnace, a kiln. 2. Curse, misfortune. بهٿ پڙنا bhaṭ-paṛnā, v. n. To be cursed, to be unfortunate, to be lost ( lit. to fall into the fire).
s. m. A warriour, a hero.
subs: To fall into misfortune, be unfortunate, be ruined or lost, to be cursed
intj. (colloq.) Go to blazes!; curse you!; hang it! rot it! (an exclamation of anger or disgust).

def: s.m. Warrior, hero; soldier, combatant; outcast of a particular tribe; barbarian.
s.m. A kind of grass.
s. f. The name of a prickly plant. (Solanum jacquini, Willd. ).
s. f. The name of a prickly plant. (Solanum jacquini, Willd. ).

s.m. = bhattā, q.v. s.m. Advances to ploughmen without interest; ploughman's wages in kind; additional allowance, extra allowance or pay to public servants on special duty, or to officers and soldiers (commonly called 'batta'); subsistence money; travelling allowance
more: n. m. H. baḍhnā , to increase. 1. A current; tide (A. mad ); ebb-tide. 2. A stone. 3. An egg plant. juār bhāṭā ; A jazr o mad , n. m. Flood tide.
adj. Agreeable; pleasing; beloved; dear. Khāīye man-bhātā, pahnye jag-bhātā . Prov. Eat what you please, dress for the world.
s. m. An extra allowance to troops on service. See بهته.

def: s.m. = bhāṭhā, q.v. s.f. Quiver (for arrows).

s.m. The egg-plant = bhanṭā, q.v. s.m. = bhattā, q.v. s.m. def: s.f. The colocynth or bitter apple.
adj. Agreeable to the mind, pleasing, charming ( = bhāyā and man-bhāwan).
s.m. One who presents bhāt; maker of presents at the ceremony of bhāt; a relative of the bride or bridegroom on the mother's side; a term of abuse.
s.f. The avocation of a bhāṭ or bard; the encomiums of a bard, praise, panegyric, eulogy; adulation, flattery; — begging, mendicancy.
more: s. f. Praise, panegyrick, the encomiums of a بهاٿ bhāṭ, which see.

def: n. f. H. bhāṭ a bard. 1. The avocation of a bard. 2. Praise; panegyric; adulation. 3. Honeyed words; incense; wheedling; flattery. 4. Begging; mendicancy; begging from door to door. Kamaulā se hāth, goṛ thaklaīn, to bhaṭaī kare laglan . Bhoj. With labouring tired his arms and legs, to begging alms given o'er.

v. n. H. bāṭ road, khonā to lose. 1. To go astray; wander; miss the right path; lose one's way; take the wrong way. Woh kāṅṭā jo dil meṅ khaṭaktā rahā, To sahra meṅ yeh bhī bhaṭaktā rahā . Mīr Hasan. 2. To wander after; to haunt (the spirit); long or pine for. Bachche ke līye jī bhaṭaktā hai . 3. To deviate; err; depart from the right.
v.n. To go astray, to stray, wander, miss the right path, lose the path or way; to err, to deviate or depart from a right course; to be restless or disquieted, to long or pine (for, ke liye).
more: v. n. To go astray, to stray, wander, miss the right path, lose (the way).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Wandering, straying, astray
subs: v.n. To go wandering about; to wander about being astray.

def: adj. Wandering; astray. bhūlā bhaṭkā , n. m. A straggler; one who has lost his way.
v.t. = bhaṭkānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to stray or wander, to lead astray; to mislead, bewilder, baulk, deceive; to scare.
s.f. A female of the bhāṭ caste; wife of a bhāṭ; female minstrel.
more: s. f. The wife of a بهاٿ bhāṭ.

def: s.m. Husband
more: W., n. m. S. भर्त्ता A husband ( khāvind ). Jawān jāë patāl, buṛhyā māṅge bhatār . Prov. Young maidens die, old crones for husbands try.

def: s.m. Name of a subdivision of the Gaur rājpūts.
s.f. Quiver (for arrows).
s.m. Flow, course; stream, current; tide, ebb-tide (see bhāṭhī).
more: s. m. A current or stream, the ebbtide.

def: s.f. Bellows = bhāṭhī, q.v.
s.f. Current, stream; down-stream; — adj. With the current ( = bhaṭhiyāl. — The word, in its Prakrit form, occurs in Sanskrit, in the compound rāya-bhāṭī, 'royal, or main, stream').
more: s. f. Down the river, with the current.

def: s.f. Bellows.
n. f. Down the river; with the current.
adj. = bhaṭhiyāl, q.v.
more: adj. Down, below, down the river, with the stream. See بهٿهيال.

def: s.m. A kind of mars̤iya or elegy sung in praise of the brothers Hasan and Husain.
adj. With the current or stream, down the river and not with the flood tide.
more: 1. adj. With the current, i. e. down the river and not with the flood tide. 2. s. m. A kind of مرثيه or elegiack verses sung in praise of Hasan and Husain.

def: v.n. To go down the river, or down stream; to ebb (the tide); — v.t. To cause to go down or sink; to ruin (a person).
more: v. n. To go down the river; to ebb (the tide).

def: s. m. A sutler, an innkeeper, one who prepares victuals for travellers in a سرا sarā.
s.m. One who keeps a sarāʼe, one who prepares victuals for travellers in a sarāʼe; innkeeper; sutler; baker.
s.f. Woman who carries on the business of an inn-keeper; wife of a bhaṭhiyārā.
s.f. Woman who carries on the business of an inn-keeper; wife of a bhaṭhiyārā.
s.m. Sarāʼe, caravansary, inn, eating-house; — low place.
more: s. m. An inn, a caravansary, an eatinghouse.

def: s.m. The business, or the status, of a bhaṭhiyārā.
more: s. m. The business of a بهٿهيارا bhaṭhiyārā.

def: v. n. 1. To go down the river; to ebb (the tide). 2. To cause to sink or go down, ruin one.

s.f. = bhāṅti, q.v. s.f. Way, manner, mode, method, style, fashion; variety, kind, sort, class; — bhāṅṭ-bhāṅṭ or bhāṅti-bhāṅti (-ke), adj. Various kinds (of), various, diverse, of many kinds or sorts, miscellaneous, multifarious
subs: bhalī-bhāṅt, adv. Well, thoroughly, fully, quite, soundly, severely, &c.
kisī bhāṅti adv. By some means

def: s.f. Down-stream, &c. = bhāṭhī, q.v.
s.f. A Mohammadan ceremony which consists in sending cooked rice or other food, for three days, to the house in which a death has taken place; the food, &c. so sent.
more: n. m. One who presents bhāt (2); a relative of the bride or bridegroom on the mothers' side; an abuse.

def: s.m. Brother's son, nephew; wife's brother's son.
more: n. m. S. भातृज Brother's son; nephew.
s. m. Wifeʼs brotherʼs son ( Qanoone-islam ).

def: s.f. Brother's daughter, niece; wife's brother's daughter.

def: s.f. Wife of a bhāṭ, q.v.; woman or girl of the bhāṭ tribe ( = bhāṭan).
s.m. Land of the poorest kind (in the dialect of Sāgar and Bundelkhanḍ).
adj. = bhaṭhiyāl, q.v.

def: s.m. Name of a class of inferior Rājpūts.
s.f The prickly plant Solanum Jacquini (used medicinally for diarrhœa and coughs: — syn. bang-kaṭīyā; ban-kaṭaiyā).

E. n. f. H. kāṅṭā thorn. Solanum jacquini ; a prickly wild plant bearing yellow flowers, used generally for checking cough in children. Bhaṭkaṭaīyā ke phūl khoṅkhī ke jaṛ kho delā . Mag. The prickly plant called bhaṭ-khaṭaīyā is a thorough cure for cough.
s.f The prickly plant Solanum Jacquini (used medicinally for diarrhœa and coughs: — syn. bang-kaṭīyā; ban-kaṭaiyā).
s.m. = biṭmās, q.v. s.m. A coarse species of pulse (of the māsh, or of the kalāya kind).
s.m. Name of a tribe of Kāyaths.
s.m. Lands allotted to bhāṭs or bards; lands granted to Brāhmans.
s.m. Learned man, scholar; philosopher (esp. one conversant with the philosophical systems); a title of Maharatta Brahmans
more: s. m. 1. A learned man. 2. A title of Mahārātta Brāhmans.

def: s.m. Breaking, separation, disunion, difference, disagreement, interruption, disturbance; betrayal; breach, rupture, fracture; fissure, chasm, cleft; separation, difference, distinction, peculiarity; discrimination, discernment; kind, sort, species, variety; device; secrecy, secret, mystery; secret or hidden virtues or resources (of)
more: n. m. S. भिद् to break. 1. Difference; variance; dissimilarity; discrepancy. 2. Secrecy; secret; mystery; secret intention or purpose. Is meṅ bhī kuchh bhed hai . Prov. There is some mystery here. 3. Problem; puzzle; riddle; enigma. 4. Knowledge; cognizance; ken. Ghar kā bhed jab hī pāyā, jab chauk puran ko ḍhaknā āyā . Prov. The house resources only then found out, When for the ' chauk puran ' the cup's brought out. (An earthen cup, instead of a brass or silver one, shows the poverty of the house). bhed pānā , v. n. 1. To find out a secret; get a clue. 2. To solve (a problem; riddle, enigma). bhed denā , v. n. To give a clue; to utter or betray a secret. bhed rakh'nā , v. n. To keep secret; keep one's own counsel. bhed kholnā , v. n. To divulge; expose; betray; unmask. bhed lenā , v. n. To spy; pry into; reconnoitre; find a clue.
s. m. 1. A secret, secrecy, mystery. 2. Separation, difference. بهيدلينا bhed-lenā, v. a. To spy, to pry into, to work oneʼs self into confidence, to sound.
subs: v.n. To find out a secret, discover the secret (of), solve (a problem, riddle, &c.)

(-kā), To keep a secret; to keep one's own counsel

(kā), To spy, pry into, get at the secret (of), worm ooneself into another's confidence, to sound.

def: v. a. 1. To shut; close ( band karnā ). 2. To pen; fence. 3. To lock up; shut up. 4. To bribe; give as a bribe. Pulis-wāle ko do rupaë bheṛe to chhuṭṭī huī .

more: n. m. S. वेश dress. 1. Appearance ( rūp ); aspect; look; likeness; resemblance; dress; guise; garb; color; complexion; phase. 1. Jaisā des, waisā bhes . Prov. The people as the country. 2. Mūlī kā sā kātlā, aur dahī kā sā bhes, Būjhe hai to būjh, nahīṅ chal bāp ke des . Riddle. Ans. Rupayā . A slice of radish like, as white as any tyre, Canst make this out? If not, to your father's home retire. 3. Tulsī jag meṅ āë-ke sab se milye dhāë, Nā jānūṅ kis bhes meṅ Nārāyan mil jāë . Dohā. O Tulsī, in this world d'you all men kindly greet, Who knows, what form assumed, Nārāyan you may meet? 2. Figure; form; shape ( shakl ). 3. Air; manner ( ḍhaṅg ); carriage; port; demeanour. 4. Show; seeming; assumed likeness; personation; disguise; mask ( bhes badal ). Bhekh barābar bhekh, gun barābar nāhe, Tol barābar gunchī, mol barābar nāhe . Kabīr. Like fair in outward looks not equal worth declares, The we…
s. m. Disguise, assumed likeness, counterfeit dress, semblance, guise, garb.
ex: دہشتگردوں نے اہلکاروں کے بھس میں، سرکاری عمارت میں گھس گئے۔
dahśatgardõ ne ahlkārõ ke bhes mẽ, sarkārī imārat mẽ ghus gaye.
The terrorists sneaked into the government building in the guise of officials.

ety: bheṭ˚ = Prk. अब्भट्टं(इ) = S. अभ्यट(ति), rt. अभि+अट्
def: v.t. To meet, come in contact (with), to embrace, to come across, encounter, come up to; to visit; to join company; to make a present to a superior (see bheṭ honā).
more: v. a. 1. To meet, to join. 2. To make a present (to a superiour).

def: v. n. 1. To touch; come in contact with ( bhiṭnā ); embrace. 2. To meet; come across; encounter; come up to. 3. To see; visit; pay a visit to. Bheṭe anuj sahit Raghu nāthā . Rāmāyan.
s.m. = bhetā, q.v. s.m. One who causes division, one who sows dissension; seditious or factious person; traitor.

more: (perhaps, for भाव ) s. m. A state or condition of being, innate property, nature, disposition.

Breaking, separation, disunion, difference, disagreement, interruption, disturbance; betrayal; breach, rupture, fracture; fissure, chasm, cleft; separation, difference, distinction, peculiarity; discrimination, discernment; kind, sort, species, variety; device; secrecy, secret, mystery; secret or hidden virtues or resources (of)

To be broken or split in pieces, be bruised or crushed; to crumble to pieces; to shrivel, shrink, collapse; to be contracted or compressed; to be coerced, constrained or compelled.

v. a. To squeeze, to compress, to crush.

def: v.t. To wet, moisten, soak, steep.
v.n. (prov.) = bhīgnā, q.v.

ety: bhīg˚ = Prk. अब्भिंगे(इ), or अब्भिंग(इ), fr. S. अभ्यङ्ग
def: v.n. To be wet, damp, or moist; to be passed in mirth and revelry (the night, rāt being expressed).
more: v. n. To be wet. See بهيجنا bhījnā. رات بهيگنا rāt bhīgnā, is applied to the night passing in mirth and musical entertainment.

def: v. n. S. अभ्यंज् to anoint. To be wet, damp, moist. Ūpar ke man gorī aisī roī, Sab bhījī roë roë chadaryā. Gorī roī jaise barse badaryā . Wom. Song. For show the maiden wept to that degree, Her mantle wet with tears all from her ee, She wept like rain from clouds continuously. maseṅ bhīgnā , v. n. To come out or grow (whiskers); just to arrive at the age of puberty. rāt bhīgnā , v. n. To pass, or be advanced (the night).
v. a. To put in water; wet; steep. bhigo bhigo-ke lagānā, yā mārnā , v. a. To make mock obeisance; eulogize in chaff.
v.t. To wet soak, steep, &c. ( = bhigānā, bhigwānā)
more: (trans. of بهيگنا) v. a. To wet, to steep, to bedew, drench.
subs: v.t. To make mock obeisance; to eulogize mockingly or in fun.

def: v.t. To cause to be soaked or drenched; to wet, soak, &c. ( = bhigānā).
s.m. Name of a tribe (supposed to be the aborigines of Ruhelkhanḍ).
part. adj. Moistened, wet, steeped, soaked; moist, damp.

ety: bhīj˚ = Prk. अब्भिज्ज(इ) = S. अभ्यज्य(ते), pass. of rt. अभि+अञ्ज्

more: adj. 1. Wet; steeped; soaked; damp; moist. 2. Drenched; reeking; dripping; soaking. bhijo'nā , Hin. n. m. Wet grain.
v. n. 1. To be wet. 2. To be affected (with grief).

ایمیل بھیجنا
to send an email
wety: Perhaps from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀲𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀤𑀺 (visajjadi), from Sanskrit विसर्जयति (visarjayati, “to send, utter”). Turner disagrees, and reconstructs Ashokan Prakrit *𑀪𑁂𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀢𑀺 (*bhejjati), possibly from a Sanskrit अभि-अज्यते (abhi-ajyate, “to be led to”). The first etymology is slightly problematic, but the changes from s to h in Prakrit is not uncommon and so it is plausible. Tedesco gives the developmen…

def: v. n. To be wet (بهيگنا). Ote sūtū, ote sūtū, nandī ji ke bhaīyā, Ki pāṭ cholyā bhīṅjahī pasenwā . Mag. Song. Sleep further off, sleep further off, now brother of my sister-in-law! my boddice is with perspiration wet.
Caus. of بهيجنا q. v.

def: to beg
wety: From بھیک (bhīk, “begging”) + مانگنا (māṅgnā, “to ask”), literally “to ask for begging”.

def: n. f. S. भिक्ष् to beg for. Alms; charity. 1. Bin māṅge motī mileṅ, māṅgī mile na bhīk . Prov. Pearls for those who ask not; who ask, not alms e'en in their lot. 2. Bhīk ke ṭukṛe, bazār meṅ ḍakār! Prov. Fed on scraps of alms, he belches in the streets. (A token of having dined well). bhīk māṅgnā , v. n. To beg alms; to beg. 1. Apne nain gaṅvāë ke, dar dar māṅge bhīk! Prov. His own eyes he puts out, then begs from door to door. (Having squandered away his own substance he now begs from others). 2. Māṅge bīhk! pūchhe gāoṅ kī jamā? Prov. A beggar! and he wants to know the rental of the village! bhīk maṅgānā , v. a. To cause to beg alms; reduce to poverty. Khetī rāj rajāë, khetī bhīk maṅgāë . Prov. Husbandry makes kings and beggars both.
s.m. = bhikhārī, q.v. s.m. Beggar, mendicant; — a proper name.
more: s. m. (dakh.) Beggar ( Harris ): see بهکهاري bhikhārī.

def: s.m. = bhīkhārī, q.v. s.m. Beggar, mendicant; — a proper name.
n. m. H. bhīk begging. A beggar ( faqīr ); mendicant ( sāiṅ ). Bāp bhikārī, pūt bhanḍārī! Prov. The father a beggar, the son a large stock owner!
